summary: Mechanical stage-system naming cleanup: the stage/ground user-data type formerly named `Map` was renamed to `Ground`, and local variables using `map` were generally renamed to `gp`. The PR touched `src/melee/gr` stage files, `ground.c`, `ground.h`, `grlib.c`, and `types.h`, updating function prototypes, casts, inline helpers, and stage-specific callback code without changing behavior.
what_changed: Renamed the stage user-data type from `Map` to `Ground` across the `src/melee/gr` code shown in the PR diff.

Updated local variables from `Map* map` to `Ground* gp` in stage object setup and callback routines, including accesses to `map_id`, `x8_callback`, `xC_callback`, `x1C_callback`, `x10_flags`, `x11_flags`, `xC4`, `xC8`, `xCC`, and `xD0`.
Updated `ground.h` prototypes from `Map*` to `Ground*` for helpers such as `func_801C5440`, `func_801C54DC`, `func_801C5544`, `func_801C5630`, and `func_801C5694`.
Updated `Ground_JObjInline1` in `ground.h` to cast `HSD_GObjGetUserData(gobj)` to `Ground* gp` and use `gp->map_id`.

Updated comments and declarations in `ground.c`, including the comment `Stage destroy map gobj` to `Stage destroy ground gobj` and declarations in functions such as `func_801C1A20`, `func_801C1CD0`, `func_801C1D38`, and `func_801C4A08`.
Applied the same naming pattern across many stage-specific files, including battle, story, shrine, figure, and target-test stage files.
smart_moves: Kept the refactor behavior-neutral: the diff is balanced at 215 added and 215 deleted lines, consistent with a type/local rename rather than logic changes.
Renamed both the type and common local variable at the same time, avoiding misleading combinations such as `Ground* map` in most shown hunks.
Used the short local variable name `gp`, which matches the existing convention visible in `ground.c` after the rename and likely stands for ground pointer.
Updated header declarations and inline helper casts along with C file usages, reducing risk of stale prototypes or mismatched user-data casts.
Preserved field names such as `map_id`; the PR only changed the broader object concept from `Map` to `Ground`, avoiding unnecessary churn where field evidence did not support a rename.
decomp_lessons: For broad terminology corrections in decomp projects, prefer small mechanical PRs that only rename symbols/types and leave behavior untouched.
When renaming a core struct/type, update all pointer declarations, casts, prototypes, static helper declarations, and inline functions in headers in the same pass.
Stage GObj `user_data` in the `gr` system should be treated as `Ground*` after this PR, not `Map*`.
A domain name that matches the source directory and file naming (`gr`, `ground.c`, `ground.h`) is preferable to an ambiguous generic type like `Map`.
Do not rename internal fields such as `map_id` solely because the containing type was renamed; preserve uncertain or still-evidenced names until separately justified.
Mechanical renames can aid future matching/decomp readability without affecting assembly, but they should be reviewed as naming/header hygiene rather than functional progress.
naming_conventions: `Map` type was replaced by `Ground` in the stage/ground subsystem.
Local variables pointing to `Ground` are commonly named `gp` in this PR.
Stage object comments should refer to `ground gobj` rather than `map gobj` when describing `Ground` user data.
The field name `map_id` remains unchanged inside `Ground`, indicating only the containing type name changed in this PR.
assembly_or_matching_tactics: This was a source-level naming refactor with no evidence of new matching tactics, asm constraints, or code-generation changes.
Balanced line additions/deletions and unchanged expressions suggest the rename was intended to be assembly-neutral.
Existing match-preserving artifacts such as unused stack arrays and `/// @todo Missing cast` comments were left intact rather than cleaned up during the rename.
review_feedback: No issue comments or review comments were present in the provided context.
The PR had one review, but no review body or specific feedback was included in the slice.
No explicit reviewer rationale for `Ground` over `Map` was provided beyond the PR title and mechanical diff.
follow_up_queries: Find later PRs that rename `map_id` or further document the `Ground` struct fields.
Search for remaining `Map*` usages after PR 703 to identify incomplete rename areas or unrelated map concepts.
Look for PRs touching `src/melee/gr/types.h` after this rename to see how the `Ground` struct was refined.
Search review history for naming discussions around `Ground`, `gr`, or stage user-data conventions.
